search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae acute sprain telehealth guideline PDF
candidate_sources_rejected: site-specific ankle-only rules as a complete source
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The workflow is not limited to ankle injury.
Opinion pages were not authoritative exact guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for grade-compatible sprains and strains suitable for virtual assessment; not exact for open injury, every joint, complete rupture, paediatric growth-plate injury, or fracture.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; partial for in-person ligament examination and non-Dubai settings.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 copy, effective 2024-04-21 and due for revision 2029-02-21, was opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: Issue 2 remains current on the DHA telehealth guidance portal.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to establish sprain and strain scope; no additional broad injury items were mapped from scope alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped sprain and strain terminology, mechanisms, symptoms, function, recurrence, and assessed findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped deformity, movement, neurovascular, severe-swelling, pop, and selected X-ray concern fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped clinician-documented RICE, activity, analgesia, return, review, and follow-up fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

unresolved_source_gaps: Open-wound, formal ligament-stress-test, exact fixed 48-hour weight-bearing, and broad joint-specific examination fields remain unsupported.
The ligament-injury label must remain clinician-stated and grade dependent.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
